title: Overnight Oats with Banana
tagline: Make-ahead breakfast jars for early lectures and low effort mornings.
steps: Stir the oats, yogurt and milk together in a lidded jar.
Slice over the banana and spoon on the peanut butter.
Chill overnight so breakfast is ready to grab in the morning.

title: Egg Fried Rice Boxes
tagline: A fast leftover-rice meal that works for both lunch and dinner.
steps: Scramble the eggs in a hot pan and set them aside.
Fry the rice with peas, spring onion and soy sauce until hot.
Fold the eggs back in and divide into lunch boxes.

title: Chickpea Hummus Wraps
tagline: A no-cook lunch that still feels filling during busy campus days.
steps: Mash the chickpeas lightly so the filling holds together.
Spread hummus over the wraps and top with chickpeas and sliced vegetables.
Roll tightly, slice in half and pack for later.

title: Tuna Pasta Salad Jars
tagline: Cold pasta pots with enough protein to stop the 3pm crash.
steps: Cook the pasta and cool it quickly under cold water.
Mix the pasta with tuna, sweetcorn, tomatoes and yogurt.
Layer into jars so lunch is ready to take out of the fridge.

title: Lentil Coconut Curry
tagline: A one-pan dinner with strong flavor and several cheap pantry staples.
steps: Soften the onion and garlic, then stir through the curry paste.
Add the lentils and coconut milk with a splash of water.
Simmer until the lentils are soft and the sauce thickens.

title: Chicken Noodle Stir Fry
tagline: Fast, flexible and much cheaper than ordering in after a long day.
steps: Cook the noodles according to the packet and drain them.
Stir fry sliced chicken until browned, then add the vegetables.
Toss through the noodles and soy sauce until glossy.

title: Yogurt Berry Crunch Pots
tagline: A snack that feels like dessert but still supports the rest of the week.
steps: Spoon yogurt into a small pot or jar.
Top with berries and granola for texture.
Finish with a little honey and chill until needed.

title: Turkey Burrito Bowls
tagline: A strong batch-cooking option when the week is packed with deadlines.
steps: Brown the turkey mince and simmer it briefly with salsa.
Warm the rice and black beans while chopping the lettuce.
Layer everything into bowls for a fast dinner or next-day lunch.

title: Tomato Soup and Toasties
tagline: Comfort food for colder evenings without spending much money.
steps: Cook the onion and tomatoes until soft, then blend with stock into soup.
Fill the bread with cheddar and toast both sides in a pan.
Serve the toasties with the hot soup.

title: Tofu Peanut Noodle Cups
tagline: A plant-based lunch that reheats well between classes.
steps: Bake or pan-fry the tofu until the edges turn golden.
Soak the noodles and toss them with peanut butter and lime juice.
Add carrot ribbons and tofu, then portion into cups.

title: Salmon Sweet Potato Traybake
tagline: A low-effort oven dinner that still feels a little more premium.
steps: Roast the sweet potato first so it gets a head start.
Add the salmon and green beans with oil and paprika.
Bake until the fish flakes and the vegetables are tender.

title: Greek Salad Pita Pockets
tagline: A bright lunch that works well when you want something fresh and fast.
steps: Chop the vegetables and crumble over the feta.
Warm the pitas briefly so they open without tearing.
Stuff the pitas with the salad mixture and serve.

title: Scrambled Eggs on Toast
tagline: A classic balanced breakfast that takes ten minutes and keeps you going until lunch.
steps: Whisk the eggs with milk and a pinch of salt.
Melt butter in a pan over low heat and add the eggs, stirring slowly.
Toast the bread and spoon the soft eggs on top with chopped chives.

title: Protein Smoothie Bowl
tagline: A thick blended breakfast loaded with protein that eats like a meal.
steps: Blend the protein powder, banana, yogurt and almond milk until thick.
Pour into a bowl — it should be spoonable, not drinkable.
Top with granola and any extra fruit you have to hand.

title: Apple and Peanut Butter Slices
tagline: A two-ingredient snack that needs no preparation and handles afternoon hunger.
steps: Core and slice the apple into even wedges.
Spoon peanut butter into a small pot for dipping.
Pack together so the slices stay crisp until needed.

title: Cottage Cheese and Oat Crackers
tagline: A high-protein snack that takes two minutes and keeps you from raiding the vending machine.
steps: Spoon cottage cheese onto each cracker.
Halve the cherry tomatoes and press one onto each.
Finish with a crack of black pepper.

title: Chicken and Avocado Rice Bowl
tagline: A high-protein lunch that works as meal prep for two days running.
steps: Season and pan-fry the chicken breast until cooked through, then slice.
Warm the rice and arrange in bowls with sliced avocado.
Lay the chicken on top and finish with lime juice and soy sauce.

title: Quick Minestrone Soup
tagline: A vegetable-packed one-pot dinner that stretches well and reheats even better.
steps: Sauté the carrots in a little oil until they begin to soften.
Pour in the stock and tomatoes, then add the beans and bring to a simmer.
Add the pasta and cook until tender, then season and serve.

title: Prawn and Vegetable Stir Fry
tagline: A fast pescatarian dinner that looks more impressive than the effort it takes.
steps: Cook the noodles and drain them, then set aside.
Stir fry the prawns until pink, then add the vegetables and cook until just tender.
Toss through the noodles and soy sauce and serve immediately.

title: Beef Mince Pasta
tagline: A reliable weeknight dinner that everyone knows how to make and nobody gets bored of.
steps: Brown the mince with onion and garlic until cooked through.
Add the canned tomatoes and simmer for ten minutes to develop the sauce.
Cook the pasta, drain and toss with the sauce.
